tasks.instruction: Read the text below. Decide if each sentence (1.1-1.4) is True (T), False (F), or if the information is Not In the text (NI).
tasks.text:
SHOULD KIDS HAVE PHONES?

I am eleven years old, and I believe children my age should have their own phones. Many people say phones are bad for kids, but I disagree. First, a phone helps you stay safe. When I walk home from school, I can call my mum if anything goes wrong. Last winter, my bus was late and I used my phone to let her know. Without it, she would have been really worried.

Second, phones are great for learning. I use mine to look up words in English and watch educational videos. Of course, parents should set some rules — for example, no phone during dinner or after nine o'clock at night. I follow these rules at home and I still have plenty of time to use my phone.

In my opinion, if children use phones responsibly, there is nothing wrong with having one.

items.statement: The author thinks children should not have phones until they are teenagers.
items.explanation: FALSE — The author is eleven and clearly states that children his age should have their own phones.

items.statement: The author once used his phone to contact his mother when the bus was delayed.
items.explanation: TRUE — The text says 'my bus was late and I used my phone to let her know'.

items.statement: The author's school does not allow phones in class.
items.explanation: NOT IN THE TEXT — The text mentions rules at home but says nothing about school rules on phones.

items.statement: The author believes parents should set rules about phone use.
items.explanation: TRUE — The text says 'parents should set some rules' and gives examples like no phone during dinner or after nine o'clock.
